violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2024

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution dispenser of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC called for service at time of inspection. PIC informed to manually add bleach until dispenser is at proper concentration. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2024

Potato salad was observed held at 49 degrees Fahrenheit in produce cooler. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview with PIC, potato salad was made less than 2 hours prior. PIC moved to another refrigerator at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2024

Beef was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2024

Eggs are served raw or undercooked to the customer's request. A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2024
Plantains were observed held at 115 degrees Fahrenheit. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2024

Potato salad packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2024

The door to the outside, located in the entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2024

At time of inspection, ambient of produce case was 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Establishment to not store TCS foods in unit until it reaches an ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. PIC called for service at time of inspection. At time of inspection, meat display case was out of service. PIC called for service prior to inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2024
